mentions.note: Cramer explicitly recommends buying as cruise line worth holding 'through thick and thin'—company to own long-term, not day-trade; better choice than Norwegian Cruise for investors with discipline.
Kramer's choice over Carnival in cruise line space for next 5 years; stronger fundamentals and operational execution.
Upscale cruise model (no kids, no gambling); all cruise lines well-run but Viking has best pricing power. Benefits from lower fuel costs and reopened destinations post-war.
Only cruise recommendation; insulated via higher-margin ships and specific non-disrupted destinations.
Upscale cruise line down 2.7%; even affluent consumer showing weakness in leisure spending.

Premium cruise operator leading category five consecutive years; quality obsession, contrarian expansion into Egypt/Great Lakes, fuel hedged at only 5% of revenue. Stock up 10% post-earnings despite consumer headwinds.
Hold long-term with CEO Torstein Hagen; stock at $78 feels like $100; don't trade it, own it.
